title: Project Pulse
summary: The continuity baseline, README clarification, topology split, and manifest contracts are now merged on main, while the active NEXUS work has shifted to the first migration seam under issue #57 and the newly activated conversation-surface program model in issue #56.
source: GitHub-first NEXUS truth sweep after PR #60 merge and the active migration-seam plus conversation-surface branch review.
nextAction: Move the first Electron-owned runtime responsibility behind a native supervisor seam without breaking the verified HTTP/UI contract, then carry the conversation-surface child-program model into future route and helper-slot activation work.

lanes.title: Project Pulse operator surface
lanes.summary: The right-rail Project Pulse and readable runtime-health surface shipped to main and now anchor operator-visible progress and readiness in the client.
lanes.nextAction: Keep the snapshot truthful as active NEXUS lanes move.

lanes.title: Desktop readiness validation
lanes.summary: Continuity validation closed cleanly after the current Electron and Node baseline passed 72 out of 72 automated checks and the operator-readability slice landed.
lanes.nextAction: Use the verified baseline as the migration source for the next runtime-topology work.

lanes.title: README direction clarification
lanes.summary: The README now explicitly distinguishes the current Electron continuity baseline from the approved native-C plus Avalonia target direction.
lanes.nextAction: Keep future topology work out of the README lane unless operator-facing baseline wording changes again.

lanes.title: First runtime-topology slice
lanes.summary: The first concrete runtime-topology record shipped to main with the native runtime core, Avalonia host, package boundaries, and interop contract made explicit.
lanes.nextAction: Turn the topology record into manifest shapes and migration-boundary decisions.

lanes.title: Runtime package manifest contracts
lanes.summary: The first explicit surface-package and helper-package manifest contracts are merged on main, and Project Pulse can now expose architecture artifacts directly in the UI.
lanes.nextAction: Use the merged contracts to pick the first migration seam and activate the conversation-surface program model.

lanes.title: Packaging and runtime topology
lanes.summary: Native-C-first topology remains the active engineering lane, and the next concrete slice is the first replacement seam that moves Electron-owned runtime supervision toward the future native core without breaking continuity.
lanes.nextAction: Define the first native supervisor seam around service lifecycle, health, logging, and startup failure handling while preserving the current web/API surface as the migration baseline.

lanes.title: Conversation-surface program model
lanes.summary: The first child-program model is now active against the merged manifest contracts so channel, forum, thread, timeline, and direct surfaces can declare helper slots and ownership-visible hosting rules explicitly.
lanes.nextAction: Define the first route-activation envelope and per-surface slot model without hiding imported-helper ownership or failure state.

lanes.title: ASCENT shared-surface placeholder
lanes.summary: Reserved integration space only. This remains intentionally parked and should not pull focus away from the current continuity and runtime lanes.
lanes.nextAction: Leave parked until the active desktop and conversation-surface lanes warrant reopening it.

lanes.title: ASCENT notification placeholder
lanes.summary: Reserved notification integration space only. It stays parked as a placeholder rather than active build work.
lanes.nextAction: Keep parked until the current NEXUS execution lanes are materially further along.
